1.content:
Hi. My name's Alexia. Alexia Jennifer Gibson. You'll be hearing that name plenty of times, under some surprising circumstances. I have brown hair and hazel eyes.

I was born to Greg and Ali Gibson, their first child. Six years later came my little brother, Will, a.k.a. Pest. I know, he means well, but he can be annoying.

Outside my family, I have two loves: Music, and Dick Newman. Dick knows how I feel about music. In fact, he and I met learning to play violin, and we're half of a string quartet. The other two are my girlfriends: Leslie Hindle on viola, and Andie Byrne on cello.

I fell in love with music at a young age. From the moment I could hold one, I started learning the violin. I won't describe the noises that came out when I started. But Mrs. MacDowall kept encouraging me, and so did Mom. Thanks to their faith and my determination, I went on to be asked to play at family gatherings.

I became a busker when I was 13. I sort of backed into it: All I meant to do was practice a piece in the park, because it was a beautiful day. Once I'm in the zone, it's just me and the music, with my violin becoming like part of my body. So it caught me by surprise when I finished, and people applauded. I was even more surprised when I found my case full of dollar bills. When I showed Mom and Dad, they said, "Well, Alexia, they gave you the money, so it's yours." Well , I guess there are worse ways for a girl to make some extra money.

Over the years, I've gotten to be friendly with the park regulars, and they look out for me. One time, I was playing, and some guy decided to help himself to the money in my case. I didn't see him do it (the zone, remember?), but somebody else did. When I finished, the guy offered me an apology, returned the money, and added some of his own.

With a life like that, you'd probably say that I was one of a kind.

Wrong.

******************************


It was a winter day in my senior year of high school when my world turned upside down.

It was a Saturday morning, so we were all home. We were talking about our plans for the day, when the doorbell rang.

I looked through the peephole. "Two police officers."

"Open the door," Dad said, "and ask them what they want."

I opened the door and stepped into their view. "Can I help you, officers?"

I was as friendly as you get, but they both turned white at the sight of me. "Could you tell us your name, please?" the older cop asked.

"Alexia Gibson. Why?"

They looked at each other, then turned back to me. "Are your parents home?"

I shrugged. "Mom, Daddy? They want to talk to you."

Mom and Dad opened the door all the way. "What's the problem?"

"Has your daughter been home all night and all morning?"

"Of course she has," Mom said.

"Does she have a twin sister?"

"Of course not. What's this about?"

"I don't know how to tell you this, ma'am," the older cop said. "I really don't know how. But... someone matching your daughter's description has been found dead in the park, and some of the people there said it was her."
1.title: How It Began

11.content:
They say the hardest thing is seeing your child laid out on the slab. I don't know about that, but I can tell you this: It's weird to see your own face on someone on the slab.

"It's uncanny," Mom said. "When the officers told us, I expected to see a passing resemblance. But she's... identical."

I got chills down my spine. If, for some reason, I had snuck out and stayed out the whole night, Mom and Dad would be looking at this body, and reaching the only obvious conclusion.

The senior officer from before came to us. "We've got the test results back," he said. "DNA and fingerprints say that's your daughter standing here."

"Well, of course they do," Mom said.

"If you'd done that to this girl," Dad added, "we wouldn't be standing here."

"That's the thing, Mr. Gibson," the officer said. "We did. And they say that's your daughter lying there, too."

******************************


As we drove home, I asked, "Can we stop by the park, Daddy?"

"Are you sure you want to go there, honey?" Mom asked.

"I'm sure, Mom. Maybe... maybe, if I go there, I'll see something that'll make this make sense."

We got to the park, and I could see where they'd found my double. The crime scene tape blocked off an area at the opposite end of the park from my way home. As I drew closer, I could hear people talking. Some were mourning me, while others were talking to anyone they could get their hands on. "If you see or hear anything, let us know," they said. "She didn't deserve this. Why should the guy that did it be comfortable?"

"Excuse me," I said.

Everyone turned.

For a moment, they were as stunned as the cops were. Then, they hugged me. A big group hug at first, then they hugged me individually. They were glad I was alive, and they had the luxury of not having to worry about the identity of that other girl.

I took out my ever-present violin, and began playing an elegy. It was the sort of sendoff I would have wanted, much more than the talk of vigilante justice.
11.title: The Morgue

111.content:
The next day, we were still reeling from what had happened. Let's face it, finding out there was a complete stranger who was identical to you, right down to the DNA, isn't something you get over quickly.

When the doorbell rang again, Mom asked, "Who could it be this time?"

"Take it easy, Ali," Dad replied, "it can't possibly be any weirder than yesterday."

I looked through the peephole. "Guess again, Daddy."

I opened the door and let myself inside. That's right, another person with my face.

The senior officer from yesterday was with her. "I guess I don't have to tell you why I brought her."

My double offered me her hand. "Hi. My name's Lexie."

I took her hand and shook it, relieved that nothing weird happened when we touched. "Alexia."

"Yeah, Lexie is short for Alexia."

"No, no: My name is Alexia."

Lexie turned pale. "What's your full name?"

"Alexia Jennifer Gibson. You?"

"Alexia Jennifer Keaton."

"Okay, this is getting weird."

"I'd say 'weird' is in the rear view mirror," the officer said. "We've identified the girl found in the park. Turned out, she had a fight with her boyfriend, and he took it too far. He tried to make it look like she was mugged, but, when he heard people were looking to kill the guy responsible, he turned himself in. Have a look at this." He held up a card in a plastic evidence bag.

I took it and looked. It was a driver's license, obviously hers. I read the name:

ROCKWELL
ALEXIA JENNIFER


"Okay," I said, "we're officially in cray-cray territory." Turning to Lexie, I said, "I guess I don't have to ask about your DNA and fingerprints, do I?"

"What the hell is going on?"
111.title: The Mystery Deepens

1112.content:
"Well, I'm afraid this is where we fall into the world of utter lunacy." Frankly, a feminine voice said. "You see, all of you are identical... too identical."

We all turned to find a short, stout older woman enter behind the police officers. She was professionally dressed, walking with the aid of a crystal-handled cane. Her grey hair was wrapped in a neat bun on the top of her head, and half-moon glasses hid her gentle blue eyes. "I am Dr. Grace Farrel." Calmly, she said. "I am the Chief Forensics Officer."

"You said we were too identical... How?" Wincing, I demanded.

Dr Farrel sighed. "Identical twins, although they look exactly alike, all have subtle genetic differences between the two." Calmly, she explained. "There are no such differences between either of you, or the dead girl in our morgue."

"What are you saying?!" Startled, Mom asked.

"Clones... " Softly, Will said.

Lexie and I turned to him, annoyed. "You are not helping!" in unison, we snapped.

"Actually, that is the conclusion we came to, as well." Startling us, Dr Farrel said. "As unlikely as it may seem, it's the only conclusion that checks off all of the boxes." She frowned. "Unfortunately, this opens a slew of other questions left unanswered."

"Why us?" Softly, I said.

Dr Farrel shook her head. "This goes far beyond just you girls." Serious, she said. "All of you have a younger brother, though each of them is different from each other."

"What are you trying to tell us, doctor?" Wincing, I asked.

"Whoever did this wanted not only copies of you girls, but wanted you all to to grow up in near identical surroundings." Darkly, Dr Farrel explained. "A little brother, a caring family, and a love for music,... specifically, the violin."

All the color drained from my face as I turned to Lexie. "You have a little brother?"

Lexie nodded, pale as well. "His name is William... "

"Six years younger?" Feeling faint, I asked.

Lexie shook her head. "No, seven years."

A sigh escaped my lips. "At least not everything is identical."
1112.title: Brother complex.

choices: A talk with the other's brother.
11121.content:
"William is in the car." Softly, Mrs Keaton said. "I'll get him."

She left the house for a moment, returning with a small boy in a sweatshirt, sweatpants, and sneakers. For a moment, his eyes darted between Lexie and myself, before turning to Will, who gave him an awkward smile.

"I have two sisters... ?" Stunned, William asked.

"They're clones." Smiling, Will said.

William was silent for a moment before shaking his head. "We're going to need another bathroom, or I'll never get in there."

Lexie and I turned to each other before giggling. "Typical boy." Bemused, we both said.

"She is not going home with us, genius." Firmly, Lexie said. "We're just here to try to figure this all out."

"At least you two aren't clones." Relieved, I added.

William turned to Will for a moment before will smiled slightly. "I'm more handsome."

William scoffed. "You are not!"

Will childishly stuck his tongue out. "Am too."

The two took a step closer, only Lexie and I held them back. "Attention hog?" Motioning to Will, Lexie asked.

I nodded. "Unfortunately." Sighing, I said. "He tends to always want to be the center of attention." I motioned to William. "Your brother... "

Lexie sighed. "He seems to think he's living in my shadow," Worried, she replied, "like nothing he does is good enough for me, Mom, or Dad."

"The class clown and the town crier." Sadly, I said.

Lexie nodded. "Comedy and tragedy."

I turned to the two boys, batting at each other with their hands as we kept them apart. Both were just starting to chuckle. "What do think we should do with them?"

After a moment, Lexie and I turned to each other. "I've got an idea... "
11121.title: Meeting William.

111213.content:
We decided to let the two of them amuse each other, and spend some time hanging out. So we went up to my room. "Maybe, with William around," I said, "Will will stay out of my room for a while."

Lexie took a look around my room. "No games?" she asked.

I shook my head. "Never got into them. I guess that's one difference between us."

She picked up a picture of my string quartet. "Your group? Any shows?"

"We put on a show for the school, right before winter break."

"So did mine." She saw my violin case. "May I play?"

"Of course."

I expected maybe a snippet of Vivaldi, or a pop tune. Instead, Lexie brought sounds out of my violin that I'd never heard anyone make deliberately.

"What on Earth is that?"

"It's called 'The Devil Went Down To Georgia.' Like it?"

"Let's just say we've found another difference."

"I guess musical tastes aren't genetic. Wanna hear something funny?"

"I could use it."

"I hear there's this busker in the park who's supposed to be pretty good with the violin. I always wanted to hear them play, but I was scared they'd be super-talented, and I'd feel like nothing in comparison."

"Well," I said, "if you had gone to the park, we would have found out about this whole clone thing sooner."

"You?"

"I know people tell me I'm good. It's nice to hear they say so to other people."
111213.title: Learning About Each Other

1112131.content:
After a while, we decided to see what our younger brothers were up to, so we headed back downstairs. When we got to the landing, we could see what they were doing: They were talking to Dick.

"This should be simple enough," he told them. "William, maybe your family will see what you're doing more positively, if you, yourself, see it that way. Believe in yourself, and others will believe in you, too.

"And, Will, just let your sister have her privacy. Just because she isn't paying attention to you at that moment, that doesn't mean she'll never pay attention to you again. Besides, what do you plan to do when she goes off to college? Follow her there and live in her dorm?"

With that, Lexie walked over and put her hand on William's shoulder. "You going to be okay, buddy?"

"I guess so," he said.

I did the same thing to Will. "How about you?"

"I suppose."

Dick looked at Lexie. Then, he looked at me. Then Lexie. Then me. Back and forth, sniffing the air. Finally, he looked straight at me. "Alexia, what's going on? Who's she? And why does she look just like you?"

"How did you know I wasn't her?" Lexie asked.

"You're not wearing her perfume," he replied.

I couldn't help smiling. Certainly, after my birthday, he remembered my perfume. "Come on, let's sit down," I said. "You'll be glad for it."

We sat on the couch, me on his right, Lexie on his left. "Okay, so what is it?"

"This is Lexie," I said. "The reason we look alike is... we're clones."

"Clones? But... isn't human cloning illegal?"

"So's meth," Lexie said, "but people do that, too."

"We don't know who's responsible," I said. "That girl whose body they found in the park yesterday? She was a clone, too. In fact, the police came to tell Mom and Dad that it was me. Only I answered the door. That's how we found out about this."

"Damn. Are you going to be okay?"

"I don't know how to answer that right now. Are... are we going to be okay?"

Dick kissed me, as if there wasn't another one of me sitting next to him. "Of course we're okay. Should we tell the others?"

It took me a moment to realize that he was talking about our friends in the string quartet. "I'll tell them, tomorrow," I said.

Dick and I kissed again, then he left.
1112131.title: Dick

11121311.content:
That night, after our houseguests had gone home, Mom came to my room. "Are you okay, dear?" she asked.

"I'm not sure I know how to answer that," I replied. "After all, who am I? What am I? Am I even a person?" I looked around the room. "Is any of this mine? Or does it belong to whoever looked like this first?"

Mom sat next to me on the bed. "I don't know exactly what you're going through, Alexia," she said. "And I don't understand how this happened. All I know is, I gave birth to you. I raised you. There's nothing else you can be to me but my daughter." She took a breath. "Did I ever tell you what your violin teacher said to me, when she said it was time for her to let you go?"

I shook my head.

"She said that so many of her students could play the notes, but not the music. The difference, she said, was something deep inside. This other person may have given you the ability to play the notes, but the music comes from your heart and soul. And those belong to nobody else but you."

I thought of Lexie playing what was music to her, but I didn't recognize it at first. Our faces were identical, but, if Mom was right, our souls were different.And, of course, Dick could tell us apart.

That raised another question. "What about Dick and me? Can I... can clones even have children? There's so much about this that I don't know."

"Well," Mom said, "Dr. Farrel gave us the answer to one of those questions: Young Miss Rockwell was pregnant. The argument she had with her boyfriend was over his responsibilities versus his hopes and dreams." She sighed. "You and Dick are taking precautions, aren't you?"

"Of course," I said. "Wait, you knew?"

"I'd like to think I know how my daughter feels about her boyfriend. And I noticed something different since your 18th birthday. Yes, I knew. And I know he loves you, but not Lexie. This is who you are, honey. And nobody else is exactly like that, or will ever be exactly like that. You may be someone else's clone, but you're your own person. Never forget that."

Mom kissed me on the forehead and left my room.
11121311.title: Identity Crisis

111213111.content:
The next morning, after breakfast, I decided to go back to the park with my violin, to try to clear my head and think things through. The usual crowd was there, sticking a little closer than before, which I appreciated in a way. I began to play my violin, placing myself into that zone where it was just me and the music. Before long, I began to hear another violin adding itself to the tune. Who is that... ?

Still playing, I looked around to find a figure approaching, as she played the same tune on her own violin. The crowd around me stood nearby, watching the girl approach. She was exactly my height, with the same features. Another clone?

I convinced myself it was just an illusion, because of the distance. Once the girl came close enough, she would take on her own identity. In that tense moment, as she did come closer, we both stared at each other, still playing the violin. She stopped first and I soon followed. "Um... my name is Alexa Jennifer Aidan." Wincing, she said.

I sighed. "I'm Alexia Jennifer Gibson." I offered Alexa to sit with me. "There are two others with the exact same face and similar name as us."

"Clones... " Pale, Alexa muttered. "That's what Dr Farrel, the chief forensics officer, told my family and me." She gulped. "One who looked just like me, err, us was murdered by her boyfriend."

"Do you have a little brother, too?" Wincing, I asked.

Alexa nodded, looking sad. "His name is Willard." Softly, she replied. "He lives with my dad in the next state."

Divorced... Wincing, I thought. Another difference...

"Honey, where did you run off to?!" Urgently, a feminine voice called.

"Over here, Mom!" Waving, Alexa called.

Alexa's mother rushed over, then stared at us in complete shock.
111213111.title: The Next Morning

1112131112.content:
It didn't take much persuasion to talk Alexa and her mom into coming home with me. Likewise, Mom and Dad welcomed them.

"One of the neighbors saw the body," Mrs. Aidan said. "He thought it would be better hearing it from him, instead of the police."

"Then I walked into the living room," Alexa added, "and gave him a heart attack."

"Sounds familiar," I said. "I answered the door, and the police turned white."

"I couldn't believe it when Dr. Farrel told us," Mrs. Aiden said. "I mean, I gave birth to Alexa. How could she be a clone?"

"It's a mystery to everyone," Mom said. "But your daughter makes four, so far. What did your husband say?"

"I'm waiting until I have more information before I tell him."

"Problem?"

"They love each other," Alexa explained. "They just don't like each other. They get along a lot better, now that they don't see each other all the time."

About that time, Lexie came in. "Hey, Alexia," she said. "Thought I'd get here before your friends come by for the explanation." She noticed Alexa and her mom. "What, another one?"

"Yep. Lexie, this is Alexa Aidan. Alexa, this is Lexie Keaton."

"Welcome to the club, Alexa. We're going to have to come up with a name. What do you call a group of clones?"

"You're joking, right?"

"Not entirely. There are four of us now. Odds are, there are more to come."

And that was when Dick, Leslie, and Andie arrived. "Hey, girlfriend," Leslie said. "Dick said you had something important to... What the hell?"

"Your eyes aren't deceiving you," I said. "Girls, this is Leslie Hindle and Andie Byrne. String quartet to be named later, this is Lexie Keaton and Alexa Aidan. The reason we all look alike is because... we're clones. And so was that girl they found in the park."

We told my friends everything we knew, unaware that Lexie's prediction was about to come true.
1112131112.title: Returning home.

11121311121.content:
It was almost 9:00 before everyone left for the night. We spent most of the time comparing notes about our respective lives. Lexie and I were particularly interested by the fact that Alexa's parents had separated, while ours had stayed together.

“I didn’t look a thing like him, so he always assumed that my mom had an affair with someone.” Pausing, Alexa said, “I wonder how he’d feel about learning he was partly right.”

*******************


I was up bright and early the next morning; before anyone else in the house, actually. Lexie and I had made plans with Alexa to meet for breakfast, to further discuss everything.

But that wasn’t for another couple hours, so I decided to go for a run to clear my mind. Running always helped to keep me grounded, in ways that even playing violin never could. It also helped that it was a good way to stay in shape.

After changing into some jogging gear, I left a note for my folks and left. I barely left the house before things started getting weird. There was a late model car parked down the road from my house; I didn’t think much of it, until it started following me.

Continuing on my route to the park, I glanced back occasionally to see if the car was still following me. Sure enough, it was. I was starting to get nervous. First I learn that I’m a clone, and now there’s a strange car following me.

Entering the park, I ran around the corner and hid against the wall. I hoped the driver would follow me, and, sure enough, they did – I heard the sound of a car door closing.

My heart started racing as the sound of footsteps got closer. Once I was sure they were right around the corner, I jumped out and shoved my stalker into the wall.

“Ow!” looking at me, the stalker said, “What the hell?”

Calming down ever so slightly, I saw my stalker was a boy around my age. He looked a little on the feminine side; even bearing a passing resemblance to me. “Who are you? Why are you following me?”

“I’m you; or at least another copy of you.”

Releasing him, in shock, I said, “Come again?”

Rubbing his shoulder, the boy said, “My names Alex Garfield; but everyone calls me AJ.” Looking at me, he said, “I’m another copy of you.”

I wasn’t sure about his story. Wanting to get to the bottom of this, I said, “I’ll admit there’s a strong resemblance between us. But how can you be another clone, if you’re a guy?”

His voice suddenly sounding identical to mine, he said, “It’s funny you mention that; because, according to my birth certificate, I’m a girl. I just never felt like one.”

Now I understood. "You're trans."

He nodded. "Not a lot of other trans people among us clones. In fact, I'm the only one I've found."

"Clearly, this cloning thing isn't an exact science. Well, you're #5. Want to meet the surviving others?"
11121311121.title: Jogging

111213111211.content:
"When you were a little girl," Mom said, as I walked in with AJ, "you brought home stray puppies and kittens. Now, you bring clones."

I shrugged. "Guess I just have one of those faces that says, 'Come with me if you look like this.'"

We didn't have a lot of time for the meeting before we all had to go to school, and AJ meant more questions, mostly to determine how well he fit the profile. "Do you play the violin?"

"I used to. I probably haven't lost the touch."

"Do you have a younger brother?"

"Nope. Only child."

That was certainly interesting. "How's your home life?"

He pointed outside. "That's been my home for nearly a year. Conservative parents and a trans boy don't mix well."

"You've been living in your car?" Mom asked, as if she couldn't possibly have heard right. "Where do you eat? Where do you bathe?"

"Wherever I can. I'm a bit of a hacker, so I go looking for other identities I can use to get a place to stay for a while and wash up. About a month or so ago, I found Alexia, here, in an ad for a performance by her string quartet. Then, I find an ad for a country group. A bunch of girls with the same face as mine? I had to find out about this."

"And I thought I had it rough," Alexa said.

"AJ," Mom said, "as soon as you finish your breakfast, you are going out to your car, and you are bringing all of your things into our guest bedroom. Then, you are going to shower and wash your hair, and you will make yourself at home. Is that clear?"

"So," AJ asked, "what all have you figured out about this?"

"Not much," Lexie said. "For a while, it seemed like this was all done deliberately, by someone who wanted us in a particular home life. Then, Alexa turns up with a divorced family. And now, you were kicked out of the house. It may be that the only link we have is... us."

It was time to head out for school, so we left as AJ started moving his things. When I got there, Mom texted me that AJ was now sleeping soundly. The poor thing didn't even make it to the shower. Mom just tucked him in.
111213111211.title: Introducing AJ to the Rest

1112131112111.content:
At school, it was decided not to talk about our little 'doppelganger club,' as it was weird enough by itself. Still, I was bombarded by questions regarding the girl who matched my description being found dead in the park. I could only shrug, not really wanting too many people knowing about our little mystery just yet. "Guess I just have one of those faces," I said.

After a few brief phone calls, we all agreed to meet at the park gazebo after we got out of school. I called Dick and he was more than happy to join us. Once we had all arrived, our parents decided to gather nearby while we talked. "This is beyond creepy." Wincing, AJ said.

"Tell us about it." Rolling her eyes, Lexie said. "Still, I'm glad to hear Mrs. Gibson took you in. How are you holding up?"

"Well, I restart school tomorrow with Alexia." Softly, AJ replied. "I can just hear the questions and gossip when they see me."

"We went over this... you are my long lost brother, and that's close enough to the truth." Serious, I said. "We just happen to look enough alike to be twins."

"That works for me." Shrugging, Lexie said. "Actually, that works for all of us."

We shared a chuckle at that, then turned to the sound of a loud gasp. There was a tall, muscular boy standing only a few feet away from us, with a look of complete shock on his face. "Alexia?!" Stunned, he demanded. "Alexia Rockwell?!"

Dick and AJ frowned before taking a stand between the boy and us. "I'm guessing you're Alexia Rockwell's boyfriend." Angry, Dick said. "The one who murdered her."

"She's still alive?!" Turning red, the boy demanded. "Where is she?!"

"In the morgue, because of you." Folding his arms, AJ replied. "These are her sisters, and I'm their brother."

"Sextuplets?!" Stumbling back, the boy demanded. he then suddenly smiled. "Well, maybe I can persuade one of you lovelies to give me a second chance... "

He moved to kiss Lexie's hand, only she pulled it away. "Get lost, creep!"

"The name is Georg De Vile."

"Take a hike!" Serious, Alexa snapped. "We are not interested!"

Georg turned red at the rejection and raised a hand at me, intending to punch me...
1112131112111.title: After School

11121311121112.content:
I closed my eyes in anticipation of what I thought was coming; only instead of feeling a fist hitting my face, I heard a thwacking sound, followed by someone hitting the ground. Opening my eyes, I saw Georg on the ground, with blood coming from his nose.

AJ was standing over Georg with his hand in a fist. “If you ever try to lay a hand on my sisters, I’ll put you in the hospital.”

Georg looked like he was going to protest, until our parents started running towards us. Seeing that, he got to his feet and ran off. Everyone gathered around AJ to thank him for his quick thinking.

The police came to take a report; leaving in a hurry once they realized Georg was involved. Apparently, he’d just released on bail, and the police now had cause to bring him in again. Once they left, everyone decided it was a good time to leave, so we all made plans to meet up this weekend.

Once we got home, I went to bring out the garbage cans while AJ went inside to take a shower. His hand was covered in Georg’s blood, but he figured it was a good excuse to take a shower.

Walking into the house after bringing the cans the curb, I saw Will walking out of the kitchen. Taking one look at me, he said, “I can’t stop thinking about how AJ and you are the same person.” Pausing, he said, “If you’re all clones of the same person, why is he the only one that’s trans?”

“I haven’t the foggiest idea, Will. Honestly, I’m still trying to wrap my head around everything that’s happened the last few days.”
11121311121112.title: AJ steps in.

111213111211121.content:
I was reading a book in bed, a little later, when AJ appeared at the door. “Hey, you mind if I come in?”

“No, of course not.” Sitting up as AJ entered the room, I said, “That shower must’ve been so refreshing for you.”

AJ looked better than he had this morning; his hair was less grimy, and his skin wasn’t as dirty. Sitting at the foot of my bed, he said, “You have no idea; I haven’t had a warm shower in over a week.”

“That’s not something you’ll have to worry about anymore.”

“It means a lot that your family would be so willing to take me in.” chuckling, AJ said, “I spent most of the day just talking with your mom; I almost forgot what it was like to have loving parents.”

I couldn’t help but notice the expression of AJ’s face change when he said that. Looking at him, I said, “If you don’t mind me asking, what happened between you and your folks?”

“Well, like I said, they’re rather conservative in their beliefs.” Sighing, he said, “They believed that homosexuality was a sin, and that people like me had a mental illness. So, you can imagine how they felt when I came out to them.”

We sat there in silence for a few moments as AJ composed himself. Once that was done, I said, “I have to say, until you spoke in your girl voice, I honestly thought you were just a male version of me – albeit a slightly feminine one.”

“Thanks; you have no idea how long it took me to perfect that voice.” Switching to his girl voice for a moment, AJ said, “I can still switch back and forth between the two.”

“I hope this isn’t inappropriate for me to ask, but have you started your transition yet?”

AJ shook his head, “I haven’t started taking hormones yet. I’ve been constantly on the move, so I haven’t had an opportunity to see a therapist.” Pulling his top out, he said, “Right now the only thing I’m really doing is binding my chest.”

I leaned forward and caught a quick glimpse of his chest binder. “Once you get settled, I’m sure my parents will be more than willing to find you a therapist.”

His face lighting up, AJ said, “I’d really appreciate that.”

Leaning back, I said, “There is one thing I don’t quite understand: how come, out of the five of us, you’re the only trans one?”

“I’ve been wondering that myself; all I’ve come up with is that maybe the cloning process wasn’t perfect.”
111213111211121.title: Talking with AJ

1112131112111211.content:
Later on, as I was getting ready for bed, Will knocked on my door. "Um, sis,... can we talk a minute?" Uncertain, he asked.

"Come in, I'm decent." Calmly, I replied.

Will shuffled in, looking a little shaken. "Lexie's little brother and I look a lot alike." Worried, he said. "Do you think we're clones, too?"

I smiled at that. "I don't think so, Will." Kindly, I said. "I mean, if you were, AJ would have had a little brother, too, don't you think?"

"I don't know... " Rubbing one arm, Will said. "AJ has me as a little brother, now, though."

"I'm sure he would appreciate that, Will." Smiling, I said. "Now tell me, what's really bugging you?"

Will sighed. "I heard you almost got hurt today." Tearing up, Will said. "I wanted to give you something, to help that not happen again."

I raised a single eyebrow. "What is it?"

Will pulled a small whistle out of his pocket and handed it to me. "It's a hyperwhistle." Serious, he said. "One breath into this, and the whole neighborhood will hear it."

"Where on Earth did you get this?" Curious, I asked.

"I bought it online the moment you and Dick started dating." Blushing, Will replied. "I, well, wasn't too sure about you being alone with anyone but family."

I sighed. I might have known. Bemused, I thought. Then I asked, "What stopped you from giving it to me then?"

"Dick saved me from a group of bullies the same day it came in the mail." Softly, Will replied. "I knew then he was okay."

"Thanks, bro." Accepting the whistle, I said. "I hope I never have to use it."

"It's better to have it and not need it, than need it and not have it." Serious, Will said.

I nodded. "True enough."

I then gave him a hug, much to his displeasure. "You're going to give me cooties!"

I laughed at that as Will hurried out of my room. It's nice to know he cares.
1112131112111211.title: Little brother's concern.

11121311121112111.content:
Living in the house did wonders for AJ. He was clean, well rested, and had a light in his eyes that wasn't there when we first met. I was certain he would start making friends in no time.

At lunch, though, he joined me and the rest of the quartet. It didn't take long for them to figure out that the "long-lost brother" story was just that. Once he figured out that they knew about the clones, though, AJ shared his story with them.

"How about we use this for our name?" Leslie asked. "We could call ourselves the ACGT String Quartet."

"ACGT?" I asked. "Do I want to know?"

"They're the letters that geneticists use when studying DNA," she explained. "It's not giving anything away. Even the folks who know what it means will think we're just saying music is in our genes."

"We'll think about it," Dick said. "What are you up to now, AJ?"

"I've set up my smartphone to search for stories about girls matching our description. I mean, it's not like we can ask some podcaster to look into it for us, right?"

As we talked, Dick and I indulged in our standard public display of affection for school: Rather than draw the hoots and hollers that followed most PDAs, I would caress Dick's free hand with mine, and he would do likewise. You had to be sitting at the table with us to see it, but we felt it conveyed just as much as one of those big damn kisses you see in the movies.

Looking next to me, I saw AJ caressing Andie's hand the way Dick and I caressed each other's. I started thinking of how to pull AJ aside so I could explain what he saw, and the significance of it.

Then I saw Andie reciprocating.
11121311121112111.title: AJ and I at school.

111213111211121111.content:
I was grateful when the weekend came around; after such a crazy week, I was looking forward to relaxing. It was almost 9:30 when I woke up; I quickly discovered that I was the last one to wake up.

Still feeling sleepy, I walked downstairs to find Andie and AJ making out on the family room couch. I don’t know why it bothered me; but seeing Andie kissing what was basically a copy of me was weird. Going down the hall into the kitchen, I saw Mom standing by the stove. “How long has Andie been here?”

“Not very long; only about 20 minutes or so.” Looking at me, Mom said, “It warms my heart to see AJ so happy, especially after everything he’s been through.”

“It’s still weird seeing them like that.” Sitting at the island table, I said, “Doesn’t Andie realize she’s essentially making out with me?”

“You’re overthinking this, sweetie.” Coming around the table, Mom said, “The only similarities that you and AJ share are on the genetic level; other than that, you’re totally different people.”

“I guess so.” Taking a breath, I said, “I’m going for a jog to clear my mind; I’ll be back in a little while.”

************************************


It was on the cold side, maybe the mid-30s, but that wasn’t enough to stop me from jogging. I was sitting on my usual bench in the park, catching my breath, when my cellphone rang. Looking at the display, I saw it was my folks calling. “Hello?”

“Hey, it’s AJ.” Pausing, he said, “You remember how I made a search algorithm to notify me if another clone was found?”

“Yeah.” My interest piqued, I said, “Did it come back with a result?”

“Yeah, it did, but the results don’t make any sense.”

Chuckling, I said, “That’s saying a lot under the circumstances.”

“According to the information I’ve found, Alexia Jessica Rogers drowned 22 years ago.”

I was stunned; but, before I could say anything, I was interrupted by a woman standing only 5 feet away from me. “Alix? Is that you?”
111213111211121111.title: The Weekend

choices: The talk does not go well.
1112131112111211111.content:
AJ was still on the phone with me, on speaker. "Mom... "

"This is not funny, young lady." Her voice quivering, the woman said. "I thought you had been killed!" she took a cautious step forward. "Please talk to me straight, not through your iPhone."

"My name is Alexia Jennifer Gibson." Speaking up, I said. "I am in the middle of a phone conversation with my brother, AJ." I frowned. "Your turn; who are you, and what do you want with me?"

"She was my mother." Ice in his voice, AJ replied. "She and Dad kicked me out of the house a year ago... I stopped being their daughter or son then."

Mrs Garfield turned pale, realizing she was talking to two different people. "I'm sorry; you look so much like her." Softly, she said. "Please... I need to talk to my daughter."

"Why should I help you?" Angry, I demanded. "You kicked him out to fend for himself, and only came looking for him after someone matching his description was found murdered in this very park."

"Your father and I divorced soon after he kicked AJ out." Tearfully, Mrs Garfield replied. "I have been searching for her ever since."

I sighed, then turned to my phone. "It's your call, bro." Softly, I said.

After a moment, AJ sighed. "Give her the phone... "

I handed Mrs Garfield the phone, which she quickly accepted. "AJ, my baby... "
1112131112111211111.title: Continue

11121311121112111111.content:
"So you and Dad are divorced," AJ said.

"I couldn't bear it anymore," Mrs. Garfield said. "It cost me my only daughter."

"But I'm not your daughter," AJ countered. "I wish you would realize that, and acknowledge it."

"It's just so hard, AJ. It... it goes against everything I've been taught."

"Like divorce? Let me hear you say it, Mom. Please."

She swallowed hard. Twice. I wasn't sure if she'd say anything.

Finally, she whispered, "My... my son." Then , a little louder, "My son."

"Now, that wasn't so bad," AJ said, "was it?"

"My son... My son... " Mrs. Garfield kept repeating those two words, crying the tears of someone trapped in a flood who hears a motorboat approaching.

"Alexia, do you think you can guide my mom here?"

"Just listen to me, Mrs. Garfield," I said, offering my hand. "But I should warn you, there's a lot more to this story than just two girls who look a lot like AJ."

******************************


Mother and son embraced the moment she walked into the house. "I thought I lost you when your father kicked you out," she said. "Then I read that story, and I thought I lost you forever. I know I can never make it up to you, AJ, but... Can I try?"

"You weren't the only one scared by that story, Mom," AJ said. "I can't be your Alix, but I can be your AJ."

"I don't know how that girl looked so much like you, or how Alexia looks like you. But I'm glad it's brought us together again."

"Well, we've got the beginnings of an explanation," I said. "But you should be sitting down before you hear it."

She sat down, and we told her what we knew about cloning.

"But I gave birth to AJ," Mrs. Garfield said. "How could a clone get inside of me without my knowing?"

"We don't have all the answers yet," I replied. "All we know is that we're alike on the genetic level."

"But we may have found a clue," Mom said. "Twenty-three years ago, a young girl with the same face as our children drowned. It's possible that she was the original."

"And AJ? Why is he... why aren't the others more like him?"

"When we find out how the clones got here, we may find that out, too."

Andie cleared her throat. "I... I suppose you and AJ will be going back, now."

"Actually," Mrs. Garfield said, "I'm not going back. But I've been living in motel rooms for a year." She turned to Mom. "Could I trouble you to let AJ stay with you for a while, while I look for a place for us?"

"It's no trouble at all," Mom replied.
11121311121112111111.title: The talk goes well.

111213111211121111111.content:
As the rest of the Doppelganger Club and their mothers began coming in, I grabbed Andie. "Can I talk to you in private?" I asked.

Once we were in my room, Andie asked, "What's on your mind?"

"Well,... I've got some questions about you and AJ."

She gave me a skeptical look. "Are you asking me my intentions?"

"Not exactly. It's just... You know he and I are both clones, right?"

"Of course, you both told me."

"Which means we're both alike."

"I'm not attracted to you, Alexia, if that's what you're getting at."

"You're not?"

"No more than Dick is attracted to AJ. Or to Lexie. Or Alexa. That's just how it is. I can't explain it more than that."

I hugged her. "It's enough, girlfriend."

And it was. With all the times I'd seen my own face this past week, I needed something, anything, to prove there was such a thing as my individuality. That I was more than just the DNA that I shared to the last marker with four other people.

Mom came to the door. "We're ready to talk theories."

******************************


Once we were all gathered, Mrs. Keaton spoke. "We've been comparing notes, and we think we can offer clues as to what this is,... or, rather, what it isn't."

"You've got our attention," AJ said.

"For a start," she continued, "we don't think it was done deliberately. There are too many variables in our families: different religions, different political leanings. In fact, all we really have in common is... you."

"If they wanted you to achieve some purpose," Mrs. Aidan added, "they wouldn't have allowed such variables. We'd all be alike."

"What if they wanted us for... parts?" Lexie asked.

"Even less likely," Mrs. Aidan replied. "Most people just don't need organ transplants. This many clones would be completely unnecessary."

"Alright," I said, "so we weren't created to do something, and we're not spare parts. What does that leave us?"

"Our best guess is... somebody screwed up. After all, cloning is outlawed, not just in America, but around the world. This many clones is bound to raise questions."
111213111211121111111.title: Andie and I Talk

1112131112111211111111.content:
Our conversation was interrupted by the telephone ringing. After checking the caller ID, Dad answered on the second ring. "Good afternoon, Dr Farrel." Calmly, he said. "I take it you have some news... "

Dad frowned as we all strained to listen. From the frown on Dad's face, it was not good news at all. "Any idea where they came from?" Concerned, Dad demanded. "I see... thank you for letting us know."

After ending the call, Dad turned to us. "Dr Farrel's team uncovered records of an alert of twenty vials of tainted influenza vaccines, a little over fifteen years ago." Darkly, he said. "Only thirteen were found and pulled, according to the report they discovered."

"What does that have to do with... " Wincing, Alexa began.

"I got a flu shot a few weeks before I learned I was pregnant." Pale, Mom said.

"Same here." Worried, Mrs Keaton added.

"Likewise." Wincing, Mrs Garfield said.

"A cloning experiment disguised as flu shots?" Wide eyed, Will asked.

Dad shook his head. "If it were an experiment, there would not have been an alert to keep an eye out for the tainted vials." Serious, Dad said. "I think Mrs Aiden was right on target; someone messed up, and their little pet project got out."

"Well, at least we have an idea of how this happened." Softly, I said. "Only why did this happen?"

"I'm afraid your guess is as good as anyone else's." Serious, Dad said. "Still, I would advise we keep this close to the vest."

"Are you thinking whoever started this is searching for us?" Frightened, Lexie asked.

Dad was silent for a moment. "As you know, AJ made a search algorithm for people matching the description of himself and the others." Darkly, he said. "It had been pinged by another computer, looking for such searches."

We were all silent for a moment, our faces turning pale. "Whoever did this knows we're searching for each other." Softly, mom said.

Dad nodded. "And now he or she is searching for all of you." He sighed. "Also,... Dr Farrell has found another clone."
1112131112111211111111.title: Unhappy News

11121311121112111111111.content:
We went to the address Dad gave us, a one-story home. "So," AJ asked, "what's the plan?"

"I don't really have one," I replied. "So far, the other clones have all come to me. This is the first time I've gone to one. You know, except for Alexia Blackwell, but she was dead already. What were all your plans?"

"Just to see if you could help me figure this out," Lexie answered.

"The same," Alexa added.

"Okay," I said, "so I guess we pretty much wing it." I knocked.

A woman answered. "May I help... you... ?" she said, as she got a good look at all of us.

"Mrs. Graves?" I asked. "I know this may look really weird, and it's going to sound weirder. But, please, bear with us. Do you have a... a daughter, about our age, we can talk to?"

Mrs. Graves nodded, and turned inside. "Alexis, honey? Some girls would like to meet you. Excuse me: some girls and a boy." She turned back to us. "I'm sure she'll be right here. Why don't you come in?"

As we went in, I heard the whine of a motorized wheelchair, and the girl who drove the wheelchair could only be Alexis.

"There was an accident in the delivery room," Mrs. Graves said. "She has cerebral palsy. Alexis, sweetie, these kids would like to talk to you."

Alexis turned to a board attached to her wheelchair. "Hello," said a woman's voice. "I think I can guess what you want to talk about."

I nodded. "As you've noticed, you and we look a lot alike. Identical, even. And you may have heard about the young girl who died in the park. By now, you're wondering how this happened."

Alexis nodded.

"As it turns out, we're clones. Genetic reproductions of an original person." I turned to Mrs. Graves. "You got a flu shot before Alexis was conceived, didn't you?"

"How did you know?"

"Our mothers all got flu shots at about the same time." I indicated the group. "One batch was recalled, but not before seven doses were administered. And we seem to be the results."

A young boy, about 12 or 13, came into the room. "Are you okay, Alexis?" he asked. "Do you need any... help... ?"

"I'm fine, Bill," Alexis said. "These kids wanted to tell me something. You can guess what we're discussing. Go be like other boys, and stop taking such an obsessive interest in me."

"A younger brother with an interest in his big sister," I said. "Well, Bill, you may as well know: Your sister and we are clones, created when genetic information was accidentally mixed up with a flu vaccine, and injected into some women. And we don't know who it was, but we think they may be looking for us. And they might want to apologize to us and ask how they can make it up to us, but I doubt it."
11121311121112111111111.title: Meeting the new clone.

111213111211121111111111.content:
"I see you have a violin with you," Alexis said. "Would you mind if I asked you to play something?"

"Not at all," I replied, opening the case. "What would you like to hear?"

"Follow me," she said, and led us to her room.

Alexis's room had a lot of customized equipment for her needs, but there was still room for two or three people to move around, in addition to her. She turned on her computer, which had sheet music on the screen. "I've heard the computer simulation," she said, "but I'd like to hear how it really sounds."

I studied the sheet music, fascinated. "Where did this come from?"

"I wrote it, with computer software."

I gulped. I always got nervous when I was playing something for the first time: now, I also had the composer listening. Finally, I focused on the sheet music, raised my bow, and began to play.

Within the first few notes, there were audible gasps from the audience, most of whom knew how to play, themselves. The tune was beautiful. If you had plunked it in front of me, I never would have guessed that the composer had never held an instrument.

When I finished, Alexis said, "I didn't think it would sound that good. Thank you."

"Thank you," I replied. "It was an honor to be asked to be the first to play this."

"For years, I would hear music, and I would cry, because I couldn't play. It seemed cut off to me. Then I found this software."

"My violin teacher used to say, 'Many can play the notes, but few can make the music.' It doesn't live in your hands, Alexis, but in your mind, your heart, and your soul. And those are forces no disease or condition can conquer. Can you arrange this for a string quartet?"

"You know one?"

"My boyfriend and I are half of one. And I'm a busker in the park. I would be honored to introduce you to the city."

"Don't you think you should answer two questions before you start making plans?" Mrs. Graves asked.

"Huh? Oh, yeah, we need to find out who cloned us. What's the other question?"

"You said that there were seven vials used to make clones. There's you; young Miss Rockwell; Miss Keaton; Miss Aidan; Mr. Garfield; and my daughter. That's six."

I understood. Somewhere out there could be one more clone.
111213111211121111111111.title: Music Appreciation

choices: We get word the last clone is in the hospital.
1112131112111211111111111.content:
"May I try something?" Stepping up to the computer, AJ asked.

Alexis nodded and watched with curiosity as AJ briefly worked with the synthesizer controls for a moment, then replayed the piece. We all stared in amazement as the program played the music as if it had come from my violin.

Alexis quickly turned to her tablet. "How did you do that?"

AJ smiled. "I, too, love the violin, but my real talent is with computers." Kindly, he said. "So long as you know the range of the instrument, make use of the automation keys, and don't play with full chords, you can make music sound just like the instrument you want to play."

"What do you mean by not playing with full chords?" Curious, I asked.

"No software can blend chords like a real violin." Serious, AJ explained. "So you play one note of the chord for a fraction of a second, followed by another, and then another. This way, when it plays, you hear the chords blending."

"That is incredible." Smiling, Lexie said.

Alexis uploaded another piece and played it through the speaker. It was beautifully uplifting and, judging by the teary smile on Alexis's face, a major step forward for her love of music. "Thank you so much!"

"When a door closes, somewhere, a window opens." Smiling, AJ said.

"You all really made my daughter's day." Smiling, Mrs. Graves said. Then a thought came to her, and she briefly left the room.

"Where is she going?" Curious, Alexa asked.

Alexis smiled. "Mom teaches music."

Shortly after, she returned with four violins. "Alexis has always wanted to play in an orchestra." Softly, she said.

We all smiled. "We'd be honored."

"I'm the cameraman." Jumping in, Bill said.

"Fine, just don't post it on social media." Firmly, Mrs Graves said.

With that said, we each took up a violin and looked at the music Alexis had uploaded, another one of her compositions. When ready, she pressed play. It was a somber but happy tune Alexis had called Music From Within. I smiled, seeing Alexis crying happy tears, hearing her music come to life. It was a happy time for all.

I returned home feeling good, and slept well that night. Only, the following day,...
1112131112111211111111111.title: Continue

11121311121112111111111111.content:
There were no get-togethers planned, so it looked like a lazy day. Hanging out with my family, lunch, and an afternoon of busking. But first, a morning jog.

It started out simple enough: waving at the kids playing, petting the dogs out for a walk. With the dogs, I wondered how they would react to meeting a clone. Were our body odors different? Or would the dogs be as baffled by us as their masters?

About the third or fourth turn, I had a hunch that I wasn't the only one going the way I was going. The last time this happened, I thought, it was AJ. But it's probably not another clone coming to say hi.

As I approached the park, I texted Dick and AJ.

AJ replied first. WU?

In the park, I replied. Not alone.

#7?

Doubtful. I could use some help.

On my way, Dick texted back.

I'll run interference, AJ added.

As I entered the park, I heard three car doors closing. Clearly, they didn't want to draw too much attention.

Fortunately, I didn't have to go too far before I saw Dick. I waved, to make it look like a chance meeting. When we got up close to each other, we kissed.

The footsteps behind me came to a halt. Clearly, Dick represented a complication they didn't want to deal with.

"I counted three," I whispered.

"Yep," Dick said. "Dressed like joggers. You know, except for the needle one pulled out of his fanny pack."

"They're definitely up to something."

AJ came. "What's up?"

"They're looking for us, and they're willing to take us. This calls for a Zoom conference."
11121311121112111111111111.title: I sense someone following me.

111213111211121111111111111.content:
Dick and AJ shadowed me home while I filled Dad in on the details of my brief encounter in the park. Upon returning home, Mom embraced us all before leading me to my computer, where a Zoom conference had already been set up with all the clones and Dr. Grace Farrel.On

As we sat at the computer, Will turned to me with a look of fear and concern on his face. "Why didn't you use the whistle?" Scolding, he demanded.

"If I had no one coming, I would have." Serious, I replied.

"As I explained after calling this Zoom conference, someone or some group is tracking you all down." Firmly, Dad said. "We must prepare to defend ourselves."

"I'll beat anyone who even comes close." Angry, Bill said. He flashed a metal baseball bat to prove his word.

Alexis rolled her eyes. "My hero."

"You can be the last line of defense." Calmly, Dr Farrel said. "For now, we need to know who we're dealing with."

"They came out of a 2019 Jaguar XF." Serious, Dick said. "They're among the most expensive luxury cars out there."

"Yet they were wearing discount store jogging clothes." Frowning, AJ said.

"Did you get a look at the license plate?" Serious, Dr Farrel asked.

"It was a personalized license plate." Frowning, AJ replied. "DAUOCEO."

Dr Farrel was silent for a moment as she turned to a computer offscreen. "It's an unregistered plate." Frowning, she said. "It belonged to a car that was totaled twenty two years ago."

"That's the clue!" Blurting out, Will said.

"What are you talking about?" Confused, I asked.

"I had been reading the articles that AJ finds with his computer search." Serious, Will explained. "Alexia Jessica Rogers drowned 22 years ago."

"What's the connection?" curious, Lexie asked.

"She drowned in her car." Serious, Will replied. "Look at the picture that goes with the article... notice the license plate."

Curious, AJ pulled his laptop computer out of his bag and cued up the article. "Oh my God... " Breathless, he gasped.

"Send us the article link." Firmly, Dr Farrel demanded.

AJ sent the link through the zoom conference. Upon seeing the image, everyone's face turned pale. The license plate read DAUOCEO.

"We need to find that last clone before these people do." Worried, I said.
111213111211121111111111111.title: A startling clue.

1112131112111211111111111111.content:
A little over a week had passed since we discovered that last bit of information. Everyone was keeping an eye out for the mystery car, while also continuing to live their lives the best they could. To that end, Andie’s parents were spending the weekend at their cabin, and invited AJ to go with them.

I was the only one currently at home, as everyone else was at some boring boy scout thing for Will. Returning from the kitchen with a sandwich, I was on the way back to my room, when I heard a beeping noise coming from AJ’s room.

Turning around, I walked inside and discovered the beeping was coming from AJ’s computer. Pressing a button to bring up the login screen, I was grateful that AJ had giving me his password before leaving. After entering the password, the wallpaper appeared, followed by a message on the screen:

POSITIVE MATCH FOUND


I pressed the button at the bottom of the message, and another window opened – this time with information taken from a police department two hours away. After questioning the legality of AJ’s accessing a police database, I looked closer at the information.

“Oh my God, it’s her!”

The police had arrested Alexandria Josephine Williams for stealing from a Walmart. Once again, AJ’s program had found another clone; only this one was in police custody.
1112131112111211111111111111.title: Searching for the Last Clone

11121311121112111111111111112.content:
Not wanting to take any risks, Dad and I went to the police department, where we were greeted by Dr Grace Farrel, along with a tall young female police officer. She silently motioned us to follow them to a room in the rear of the building.

"First, my name is Detective Carmen Angeles." Calmly, she said. "Dr Farrel and I are in charge of this investigation."

"Why haven't we met you before?" Curious, I asked.

"There was no need to question any of you." Calmly, Carmen replied. "However, due to recent events, that might change."

"What are you saying?" Concerned, Dad demanded.

"I'll explain in here." Opening a door, Carmen replied. "There is a lot more going on with this last clone than just another pretty face."

Curious, we entered a dark room with a large window looking into another room. There, we found the seventh clone. She seemed very frightened, and it was obvious from her red and puffy eyes that she had been crying for a long time.

"What did she do?" Wincing, I asked.

"She was running from two men in jogging clothes, attempting to abduct her." Softly, Dr Farrel replied. "Sound familiar?"

"We brought her in on charges of disturbing the peace." Calmly, Carmen added. "The two men who were chasing her slipped away, but we have confiscated security footage of the chase."

"From what we have been able to get out of her, these guys tried to abduct her outside of the police station, when her family went there to inform them she was alive and well." Serious, Dr Farrel said. "She ran away in a blind panic, and has been running from the goons since."

"Poor kid." Wincing, I said. "I take it you want me to talk to her."

Carmen nodded. "We need answers, but she stopped talking after being given the clone story." She sighed. "Honestly, who could blame her... "

Minutes later, Dr. Farrel walked into the interrogation room where Alexandria was being held, with me following. Alexandria had her back to us. "I'm not talking to you." Tearfully, she said. "I want real answers."

"You and me both." Calmly, I said.

Alexandria spun around, startled. She stared at me with her mouth half open and her eyes wide as saucers. "Who - Who are you?!"

"My name is Alexia Jennifer Gibson." Calmly, I replied. "As you can see, we have a lot to talk about."
11121311121112111111111111112.title: Visiting the Police

111213111211121111111111111121.content:
I sat down at the table. "My name's Alexia," I said. "Should I call you Alexandria, or Alex, or Sandy, or - "

"My mom and dad always called me Sandy," she replied. "If only they could be here. If I dared to see them again."

"I'd like that for you, too, Sandy. But first, I need to lay all the cards on the table for you, so you know as much as the rest of us." I presented her with the first picture. "Alexia Rogers. She got into a car accident and drowned."

I put down the next picture. "Alexia Rockwell. Killed by her boyfriend. She got pregnant, he got scared."

I didn't need to add my picture to the pile, but I did. "That's me. Alexia Gibson."

The next picture. "Lexie Keaton."

Next. "Alexa Aidan."

And another one. "AJ Garfield. It's for him to explain, but he's pretty open about it."

The last one. "Alexis Graves. Which brings us to you."

Sandy looked at the photos, trying to comprehend. "How... how could this happen? How could any of this happen?"

"Like Dr. Farrel and Detective Angeles have been saying: The seven of us were cloned from the original." I pulled out the first picture. "Alexia Rogers drowned more than 20 years ago. Time enough for someone to figure out how to clone her. But they screwed up: the formula for creating clones got mixed up with flu vaccine. Whoever did it recalled it, but not before seven women were injected with it. The results: When one is found dead in the park, a few mothers get the scariest call they can imagine, and six young people start questioning everything they know about themselves. And then, mysterious goons start trying to abduct people matching a very specific description."

Sandy absorbed this, then turned to Dr. Farrel. "I want my mom."

Right then, Detective Angeles opened the door, and a woman came in. "Oh, Sandy," she said, making a beeline for her. "Oh, it's so good to see you again, baby. I missed you so much. I didn't know what to think."

Then she looked at me. "What in the world... ?"

"That's your daughter, alright, Mrs. Williams," I said, "but, as you can see, there's more to this case than meets the eye. She ran away because she was afraid for you. And now, you need to know what's going on, and what you're up against."
111213111211121111111111111121.title: Talking with Alexandria.

15.content:
Alexia Gibson: Main character; 18; plays the violin; finds out that she is a clone
Greg Gibson: Alexia's father
Ali Gibson: Alexia's mother
Will Gibson: Alexia's younger brother; 12; enjoys attention, even if it means annoying Alexia
Dick Newman: Alexia's boyfriend; 18; plays the violin
Leslie Hindle: Alexia's girlfriend; 18; plays the viola
Andie Byrne: Alexia's girlfriend; 17; plays the cello
Lexie Keaton: 18; a clone, like Alexia
William Keaton: Lexie's younger brother; 11; seeks approval
15.title: Cast of Characters

info.brief_description: What if you weren't the only one of you?
info.description:
This is the story of Alexia, who finds out that she is one of several clones.

Of course, you've gotta have rules:

1) Don't kill off the main character.
2) Try for correct spelling and punctuation.
3) Keep it PG-13. Nudity is okay. Strong language is okay, but don't overdo it. Sex is okay, but don't go into detail.
4) If you have any questions, ask me, not someone else who contributes.
5) If you're not sure what flies and doesn't fly with me, check out my other interactives.
